records.ety: [L. incessans, -antis; pref. in- not + cessare to cease: cf. F. incessant. See Cease.]

defs: Continuing or following without interruption; unceasing; unintermitted; uninterrupted; continual; as, incessant clamors; incessant pain, etc.
quotes: Against the castle gate, . . . Which with incessant force and endless hate, They batter'd day and night and entrance did await.
